title: Model and query for columnar stores
description: Exploit columnar storage by pruning columns, partitioning, and clustering so warehouse scans stay cheap.
body: Use when queries scan large fact tables on a columnar warehouse. Do not write SELECT * or filter on unpartitioned high-cardinality columns — columnar engines bill and slow on bytes scanned, not rows. Actions: select only needed columns, partition or cluster on the common filter/date key, and pre-aggregate hot rollups; inspect the query plan and bytes-scanned. Validation signals include a query plan showing partition pruning and a bytes-scanned drop after clustering. Risk boundary: an unpartitioned full-table scan can run up large cost on each run; if a frequent query scans the whole table, treat it as a cost risk and escalate to add partitioning before it recurs at scale.
